title: Refund & Return Policy
intro: Liquidation Pallet Deals sells inspected, condition-graded pallets and truckloads. Most inventory is sold "as is, where is," but we stand behind every shipment with a 30-day claim window covering shortages, transit damage, and pallets substantively misrepresented in the Listing. This page consolidates everything you need to know about claims, refunds, and pickup terms.

sections.heading: 30-Day Claim Window
paragraphs: You have 30 days from the date your pallet is delivered (or picked up) to file a claim. After that window closes, all sales are final and the inventory is considered accepted as is, where is.

We keep the window simple on purpose: 30 days is enough time to break down a pallet, inspect the contents against the Listing, and surface any real issues — without leaving claims open indefinitely on liquidation goods.

sections.heading: What's Covered
paragraphs: We approve claims that fall into one of three categories. If your situation fits, file with us and we'll work it out.
list: Documented shortages — the pallet arrived with fewer units, missing categories, or short weight versus what the Listing described.
Damage in transit — product or pallets damaged between our Jacksonville dock and your delivery address (see Freight Damage below for how title and carrier responsibility work).
Substantively misrepresented Listings — the pallet's actual contents, category mix, or condition grade is materially different from what was advertised at the time of sale.

sections.heading: What's Not Covered
paragraphs: Liquidation is a margin business, not a retail business. The following situations are not eligible for refunds, returns, or claims:
list: Buyer's remorse or change of mind after purchase.
Cosmetic mismatches, manifest variance, or condition issues that were disclosed in the Listing (mixed grades, customer returns, shelf-pulls, scratch-and-dent, etc.).
Items that didn't sell as well as you hoped, or resale margins that didn't hit your target.
Damage caused after delivery — once the pallet is on your floor, it's yours to handle.
Manifest items that aren't functional, when the Listing already disclosed the pallet as untested, customer returns, or salvage.

sections.heading: How to File a Claim
paragraphs: Send your claim to info@liquidationspalletdeals.com within 30 days of delivery. Include the following so we can move quickly:
list: Your order number and the date of delivery or pickup.
A clear description of the issue (shortage, transit damage, or misrepresented Listing).
Photo and/or video evidence — the pallet as received, any damaged units, shrink wrap condition for transit claims, and a count if you're claiming a shortage.
For freight damage: photos of the trailer, bill of lading, and any notations made with the driver at delivery.

sections.heading: Resolution & Refunds
paragraphs: Approved claims are resolved at our discretion in one of three ways: a replacement pallet or units, a partial refund, or store credit toward a future order. We pick the option that best fits the size and nature of the claim, and we talk it through with you before finalizing.

There is no restocking fee on approved claims. Refunds are issued back to the original payment method (wire, Zelle, Apple Pay, or Chime) once the claim is closed. Store credit, when issued, never expires.

sections.heading: Freight Damage & Carrier Claims
paragraphs: Title to the goods transfers to the buyer when the carrier picks up at our Jacksonville facility. That means the carrier — not LPD — is legally responsible for damage that happens in transit.

That said, we don't leave you on your own. If your delivery shows transit damage, note it on the bill of lading before the driver leaves, take photos immediately, and email us. We'll help you file the carrier claim and supply the original packing documentation and load photos from our dock.

sections.heading: Local Pickup: 5-Day Removal Window
paragraphs: If you're picking up at our Jacksonville facility (5061 Shawland Rd), you have 5 days from the date the order is marked ready to remove the pallets. We'll email and text you when it's ready to go.

Pallets left on the dock past the 5-day window are subject to storage charges and a 10% restocking fee. We can't hold inventory indefinitely — the floor space is needed for incoming loads. If you need extra time, just reach out before the window closes and we'll work with you.

sections.heading: Questions Before You Buy
paragraphs: If you're unsure whether a pallet fits your business, ask before you wire. Email info@liquidationspalletdeals.com or text +1 (656) 999-2368 on WhatsApp and we'll walk you through the manifest, condition grade, and what's typically in the mix. Clarity up front beats a claim on the back end every time.
